rationale: This is a canonical textbook example of the Wolff-Kishner reduction, showing the complete deoxygenation of a simple, acyclic, aliphatic ketone (3-hexanone) to the corresponding alkane (n-hexane). The reactants and products are simple, RDKit-parseable, and clearly illustrate the core transformation of C=O to CH2 under basic conditions (hydrazine and KOH).
collision_avoidance_note: This example uses a simple, acyclic, aliphatic ketone to avoid overlap with other named reactions. It avoids aromatic rings (common in Clemmensen reductions), esters (avoids Bouveault-Blanc), and other functional groups that might trigger different named reaction classifications. The transformation is a clear deoxygenation of a ketone to an alkane, specific to the Wolff-Kishner conditions.
